title: Metric Linking UX Round 2 — Enhanced Dropdown and Summary Sentence
story: As a data analyst, I want the metric linking dropdown to clearly show all six relationship options grouped by direction, with a dynamic summary sentence, so that I can easily understand and confirm the relationship I'm creating.
description:
Major UX enhancement to the metric linking interface:

1. **Enhanced dropdown**: Uses Bootstrap advanced dropdown with headers grouping 6 relationship options under 'Has influence on' (included in calculation of, segment of, directly impacts) and 'Is influenced by' (includes in the calculation, segmented by, impacted by)
2. **Dynamic label**: Dropdown label updates based on the selected option to reinforce the direction
3. **Summary sentence**: Below the dropdowns, a concatenated sentence reads: '[Metric Name] is [Selected Relationship] [Linked Metric]'
4. **Metric name display**: The current metric name appears above the first dropdown for clear context
5. **Hide empty sections**: If no relationships exist for 'influence on' or 'influenced by', the corresponding section is hidden to reduce noise

Although 6 options are presented, only 3 relationship types exist in the data model — the inverse options (influenced by) swap the parent/child direction in the junction table.
acceptance_criteria: Dropdown shows 6 options grouped under 'Has influence on' and 'Is influenced by' headers
Selecting an option updates the dropdown label dynamically
Summary sentence displays below: '[Metric] is [relationship] [Linked Metric]'
Current metric name displays above the relationship dropdown
Empty influence-on or influenced-by sections are hidden
Inverse options (e.g., 'segmented by') correctly swap parent/child in the junction table
ux_changes: Bootstrap advanced dropdown with grouped headers, dynamic label, concatenated summary sentence, and conditional section visibility.
middleware_changes: Inverse relationship options swap parent/child IDs when saving — selecting 'segmented by' from Metric A creates a link where Metric A is the child.
api_changes: Link creation endpoint handles direction swapping based on the selected relationship option.
implementation_notes: Required a production rollback after an intermediate version was pushed prematurely. The 6-option display maps to 3 underlying relationship types with direction inversion. Top-to-bottom reading flow emphasized for clarity.
comment_summary: Multiple rounds of refinement. Initial implementation confused the dropdown content with display format. Required rollback after production push. Final version implements grouped dropdown with dynamic labels and summary sentence. Customer-reported issue triggered the rollback.
